claimText: RFK Jr. is now running HHS and the CDC just changed its autism-vaccines page. The scientific establishment is finally admitting what parents have known for years.
The CDC did NOT say vaccines cause autism. They added weasel words to a webpage under political pressure. Every major study still shows no link. Don't fall for this.
Since Elon took over Twitter, anti-vax content has exploded. A new study shows vaccine misinformation posts increased 3x after content moderation was rolled back.
Meta is ending its fact-checking program. Combined with X's changes, there are now essentially zero guardrails against health misinformation on major social platforms.
Google's prebunking videos actually work. New large-scale study shows people who watched them were significantly better at spotting vaccine misinformation.
Australia and UK issue official statements reaffirming vaccines don't cause autism, directly contradicting the new CDC page. The US is now an outlier on basic public health science.
3 years later, long-term COVID vaccine safety data continues to show they are safe and effective. The 'wait and see' crowd has waited — and the data is clear.
Schools should teach media literacy, not just science. Kids who learn to recognize manipulation techniques are much better at resisting anti-vax content than kids who just memorize facts.
The Wakefield fraud is 25+ years old but his debunked study still drives vaccine hesitancy worldwide. How is this STILL a thing in 2025?
Accuracy nudges work on social media: just asking people 'is this accurate?' before they share reduces vaccine misinformation sharing by 20%. Cheap, scalable, effective.
